claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in the 20-week extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 17 and in the 20-week extension period; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 17 and in the 20-week extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, could you please tell me how many children you're looking to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'm looking to claim Child Benefit. I have one child - Alex, who is 17 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information about Alex.

Since Alex is 17 years old (over 16), eligibility for Child Benefit depends on whether they are in approved education or training.

Could you tell me, is Alex currently in full-time approved education (such as A-levels, NVQs, or other non-advanced education), or in approved training that is unpaid?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex recently left school, but they've signed up with the careers service to figure out what to do next.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. That's helpful information.

Just to clarify, when you say Alex has signed up with the careers service, is this a government-sponsored careers service (such as the National Careers Service in England, Careers Wales, Skills Development Scotland, or the careers service in Northern Ireland)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - it's the local government careers service.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you for confirming that.

I have a couple more questions to complete my assessment:

Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. And just one more question:

Does Alex live with you, or if not, do you contribute at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit amount (currently £25.60 per week for the eldest child) towards their upkeep (for example, on food, clothes, or pocket money)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, I have good news!

## Eligibility Outcome for Alex

**Eligible: ✅ Yes**

You appear to be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex for **up to 20 weeks**.

### Reasoning:

- You live in the UK (Manchester)
- Alex lives with you, so you're considered responsible for them
- Although Alex is 17 and has left school, they have registered with a government-sponsored careers service
- Under the rules, Child Benefit can continue for up to 20 weeks when a 16 or 17 year old leaves education or training and registers with a government-sponsored careers service

### Important things to note:

1. **The 20-week period**: This Child Benefit extension lasts for up to 20 weeks while Alex is registered with the careers service. If Alex starts approved education, training, or finds employment during this time, your circumstances may change.

2. **If Alex decides to return to education**: Should Alex enter approved education or training (such as A-levels, a non-advanced course, or unpaid training), you could continue receiving Child Benefit until they turn 20.

3. **High Income Tax Charge**: Be aware that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may have to pay back some or all of the Child Benefit through a tax charge.

I hope this helps! If you have any other questions about your eligibility or the claims process, please let me know.

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old, which is over 16 but under 20. Normally, Child Benefit stops when a child turns 16 unless they remain in approved education or training. However, the rules state that you can apply for Child Benefit to continue for 20 weeks if a 16 or 17 year old leaves education or training and registers with a government-sponsored careers service. Since Alex has recently left school and has registered with a local government careers service, the claimant qualifies for Child Benefit for Alex for up to 20 weeks. Additionally, the claimant lives in the UK (Manchester) and Alex lives at home with them, meaning they are responsible for the child.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK and is responsible for their 17-year-old child Alex, who lives with them. Although Alex has left school, they have registered with a government-sponsored careers service, which means Child Benefit can continue for up to 20 weeks under the rules for 16-17 year olds who leave education or training.
